definition:
1. a pattern  or model  of the thing to be executed, Lat. exemplar, an architect's plan , (Herdotus Historicus); a sculptor's or painter's model , (Plato Philosophus) 
2. a precedent, example , (Thucydides); ἐπὶ παραδείγματος by way of example , (Aeschines Orator) 
3. an example , i. e. a lesson  or warning , π. ἔχειν τινός to take a lesson  from another, (Thucydides); τὸ σὸν π. ἔχων (Sophocles Tragicus); ζῶντά τινα τοῖς λοιποῖς π. ποιεῖν (Demosthenes Orator) 
4. an argument, proof from example , (Thucydides) 
5. the model  or copy  of an existing thing, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
